notes:
Generated Codex variant renders from fastfn-server/videos/record_login_dashboard_codex.py using helpers_codex.py. Outputs: /tmp/fastfn-videos/login-dashboard-codex/login-dashboard-chirp_f.mp4, /tmp/fastfn-videos/login-dashboard-codex/login-dashboard-kore.mp4, /tmp/fastfn-videos/login-dashboard-codex/login-dashboard-zephyr.mp4. Voice tuning: chirp_f/kore/zephyr, improved pacing and less choppy narration.
Codex guide overhaul applied using internet-derived tutorial standards. Added videos/VIDEO_GUIDE_CODEX.md and codex scripts/helpers. Latest login-dashboard-kore.mp4 now has measured max silence ~2.855s (previously >7s), total silence reduced to ~18.2s.
Single-review cut generated on request: /tmp/fastfn-videos/login-dashboard-codex/login-dashboard-chirp_f.mp4 (Codex improved script).
Codex pass (Mar 2): updated videos/helpers_codex.py to remove synth-latency dead-air in narrate(wait=True) by sleeping only remaining audio time; updated videos/record_login_dashboard_codex.py with larger 1600x980 viewport, expanded intro pitch (Lambda/OpenFaaS/Knative/Firecracker), wildcard endpoint selection/fill before Execute, and denser dashboard narration. New render: /tmp/fastfn-videos/login-dashboard-codex/login-dashboard-chirp_f.mp4.
Codex follow-up: added OpenAPI server rewrite in recorder to runtime base (FASTFN_RUNTIME_BASE default http://127.0.0.1:8080) plus Chromium flags to avoid CORS block during capture; wildcard is auto-filled before Execute; capture now shows Curl + Request URL + Server response + body/headers in-frame. Latest output: /tmp/fastfn-videos/login-dashboard-codex/login-dashboard-chirp_f.mp4
Final tuning pass: added non-blocking narration in OpenAPI transition to avoid dead air; verified silencedetect in 15-24s window now only short gaps (0.894s and 0.729s). Final render includes full Swagger execution panel (curl, request URL, server response, body, headers) with wildcard field populated. Output: /tmp/fastfn-videos/login-dashboard-codex/login-dashboard-chirp_f.mp4
Repair pass: kept same demo endpoint family (demo-user/hello-python), repaired app.py via dashboard save endpoint, set config timeout_ms=30000 to avoid runtime 504, and enforced 2xx live status in recorder before accepting the Swagger result. Also removed wait=False narration calls to avoid voice overlap/stacking. New render: /tmp/fastfn-videos/login-dashboard-codex/login-dashboard-chirp_f.mp4 with Server response 200 visible.
title: Video: Login & Dashboard Home

close_reason: Fixed by reading schedule configs from catalog runtimes data and merging with scheduler state
description:
The schedules page only showed functions that appeared in the /_fn/schedules runtime state endpoint, missing newly added or paused schedules.

## Root Cause
- `schedules_view` only cross-referenced `functions_for_user()` with `client.schedules()`
- `client.schedules()` returns scheduler runtime state (last_run, next_run) — functions with schedule config but no scheduler state were invisible
- `catalog_for_user()` didn't work because it read `data.get('functions', [])` but catalog nests functions under `runtimes.{runtime}.functions`

## Fix
- Now reads catalog directly and extracts schedule configs from `runtimes.{rt}.functions[].policy.schedule`
- Merges config schedules with scheduler state
- Shows all functions with schedule config (enabled or paused)
- Added 'paused' badge for disabled schedules, 'cron' vs 'interval' badges

## Also Added
- 'Add Schedule' form: pick unscheduled function, set cron/interval, save inline
- 'Remove' button per schedule row (sends `{schedule: null}` to config endpoint)
title: Fix: Schedules page was only showing scheduler-state entries

close_reason: Implemented: gateway + invoke inject secrets, example function + Phase 11 e2e test
description:
Secrets stored via the dashboard Secrets page are now injected into function execution events as event.secrets.

## Changes
- openresty/lua/fastfn/http/gateway.lua: inject secrets map into event.secrets before calling daemon
- openresty/lua/fastfn/console/invoke_endpoint.lua: inject secrets into Test/invoke events
- docker-compose.yml: mount Lua sources for dev hot-reload
- srv/fn/functions/python/demo-user/secret-demo/: example function reading event.secrets
- tests/integration/test-api.sh: Phase 11 — full secrets lifecycle e2e test

## How it works
1. User creates secrets via Dashboard → Secrets page (or /_fn/secrets API)
2. Secrets stored in ngx.shared.fn_cache (sys:secret:val:{key})
3. On every function call, gateway reads all secrets and puts them in event.secrets
4. Function reads event.get('secrets', {}).get('MY_KEY')

## Access pattern
- Python: event.get('secrets', {}).get('KEY')
- Node: event.secrets?.KEY
- Lua: event.secrets and event.secrets.KEY
- PHP: $event['secrets']['KEY']
title: Feature: Secrets vault injection into function events
Dashboard: build artifacts support. Add artifact storage for compiled outputs — Go binaries, Rust binaries, Node bundles. Show artifact size, download link, and build metadata per deployment.
Benchmark Go persistent runtime with Go >=1.21 native host

description:
Build a FastFN function that checks Google Calendar for upcoming events and sends reminders via WhatsApp (web JS library, not official API) and Telegram Bot API.

## Requirements

### Google Calendar Integration
- Use Google Calendar API to fetch upcoming events
- OAuth2 or service account authentication
- Check for events within a configurable time window (e.g., 15min, 30min, 1hr before)

### Telegram Reminders
- Use Telegram Bot API (already proven in telegram-cron-node and telegram-ai-chat functions)
- Send formatted reminder messages with event title, time, location, and link
- Support configurable chat_id via secrets/env

### WhatsApp Reminders (Web JS)
- Use a JS-based WhatsApp Web library (e.g., whatsapp-web.js or Baileys) — NOT the official WhatsApp Business API
- Send reminder messages to configured phone number(s)
- Handle session persistence for WhatsApp Web authentication

### Implementation
- Create as a scheduled function (cron) that runs every 5-15 minutes
- Runtime: Node.js (best fit for whatsapp-web.js ecosystem)
- Store API keys, bot tokens, and phone numbers as secrets
- Both channels (Telegram + WhatsApp) should be optional — user enables whichever they want via env/secrets
- Include fn.config.json with schedule configuration

### Function Structure
```
examples/functions/node/calendar-reminder/
├── index.js          # Main handler
├── calendar.js       # Google Calendar API wrapper
├── telegram.js       # Telegram Bot sender
├── whatsapp.js       # WhatsApp Web sender
├── package.json      # Dependencies
└── fn.config.json    # Schedule config
```

### Dependencies
- googleapis (Google Calendar)
- node-telegram-bot-api (Telegram)
- whatsapp-web.js or @whiskeysockets/baileys (WhatsApp Web)

### Secrets needed
- GOOGLE_CALENDAR_CREDENTIALS (service account JSON or OAuth token)
- GOOGLE_CALENDAR_ID (calendar to monitor)
- TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN
- TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID
- WHATSAPP_PHONE_NUMBER (recipient)
- REMINDER_MINUTES_BEFORE (default: 15)

### Notes
- whatsapp-web.js requires a Chromium session — evaluate if this works within FastFN daemon constraints or needs a persistent process
- Consider Baileys as lighter alternative (no browser needed)
- Include example .env.example with all required variables
title: Create calendar reminder function (WhatsApp + Telegram)
